title: Mesh Topology Concepts
learning_objectives: Understand the fundamental mesh data structures in OpenFOAM (points, faces, cells)
Explain the owner-neighbor addressing scheme and its importance in CFD
Differentiate between primitiveMesh, polyMesh, and fvMesh hierarchies
Analyze how mesh topology affects numerical accuracy and computational efficiency
Identify common boundary condition types through mesh patches

concepts: Points: 3D coordinate storage using point class
Cells: Volume regions using cell class (labelList)
Key primitiveMesh methods: nPoints(), nFaces(), nCells()
Mesh addressing: owner and neighbor face lists
Cell zones, face zones, point zones for mesh partitioning
Cell volume and face area storage
Mesh motion and topological change handling
Common patch types: wall, cyclic, processor, symmetry
Boundary condition implementation in field interpolation

key_classes.name: fvMesh
key_classes.base_class: public polyMesh, public lduMesh, public surfaceInterpolation

implementation.compilation_instructions:
OpenFOAM applications compiled with:
mkdir -p $FOAM_APP/bin
wmake $FOAM_APP/bin/myMeshInfo
implementation.testing_procedure:
Test with cavity tutorial case:
cavityFoam > mesh_info.log
grep -A 5 -B 5 mesh_info.log

concept_checks.question: What is the purpose of owner-neighbor addressing in finite volume meshes?
concept_checks.answer: Owner-neighbor addressing determines which cells share each internal face, enabling flux calculations between adjacent control volumes. Each internal face has exactly one owner cell and one neighbor cell, while boundary faces have only an owner cell.
concept_checks.verification: Check mesh.faceOwner() and mesh.faceNeighbour() return lists of size nFaces()

concept_checks.question: How does polyMesh differ from primitiveMesh?
concept_checks.answer: primitiveMesh provides basic topological data (points, faces, cells), while polyMesh adds mesh boundaries, zones, parallel distribution, and time-varying capabilities. polyMesh inherits from objectRegistry and manages mesh data across time steps.
concept_checks.verification: Verify polyMesh.H shows inheritance from objectRegistry and boundaryMesh() method

concept_checks.question: What is lazy evaluation in OpenFOAM mesh data?
concept_checks.answer: Mesh data like cell volumes and face areas are computed on-demand when accessed, not stored permanently. This conserves memory but means subsequent accesses incur computational cost.
concept_checks.verification: Call mesh.V() multiple times - first access computes, subsequent access retrieves cached value

concept_checks.question: How do patches enable boundary condition implementation?
concept_checks.answer: Patches group boundary faces by type (wall, cyclic, etc.) and provide communication protocols for parallel processing. Each patch knows its neighbors and how to exchange boundary values.
concept_checks.verification: Check wallPolyPatch inherits from polyPatch and has specific wall treatment methods

concept_checks.question: Why does fvMesh inherit from multiple base classes?
concept_checks.answer: fvMesh combines polyMesh (topology), lduMesh (linear algebra addressing), and surfaceInterpolation (surface field operations) to provide complete FV functionality in a single class.
concept_checks.verification: Verify fvMesh.H shows: public polyMesh, public lduMesh, public surfaceInterpolation
